1.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus because God wanted it, sent to tell about the promise of life that is in Christ Jesus.
To Timothy, my dear son: May God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord give you grace, mercy, and peace.
I thank God, whom I serve as my ancestors did, with a clean conscience. Night and day I never stop remembering you in my prayers.
I remember your tears, and I long to see you, so that I may be filled with joy.
I remember your honest faith. That same faith first lived in your grandmother Lois and your mother Eunice, and I am sure it lives in you too.
For this reason I remind you to keep on fanning into flame the gift God gave you when I laid my hands on you.
For God did not give us a spirit that makes us afraid. He gave us a spirit of power, love, and a clear mind.
So do not be ashamed to tell others about our Lord, and do not be ashamed of me, his prisoner. Instead, join with me in suffering for the good news, by the power of God.
God saved us and called us to a holy life — not because of anything we did, but because of his own plan and grace. He gave us this grace in Christ Jesus before time began,
and now he has shown it to us through the coming of our Saviour, Christ Jesus. Christ has destroyed the power of death and, through the good news, has brought life that never ends out into the light.
I was chosen to be a preacher, an apostle, and a teacher of this good news.
That is why I am suffering as I am. But I am not ashamed, because I know the One I have trusted, and I am sure that he is able to guard what I have given into his care until that day.
Hold on to the pattern of sound teaching that you heard from me, with the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
Guard the good thing that was given to you. Guard it with the help of the Holy Spirit, who lives in us.
You know that everyone in the province of Asia has turned away from me, including Phygelus and Hermogenes.
May the Lord show mercy to the family of Onesiphorus, because he often cheered me up and was not ashamed that I was in chains.
Instead, when he was in Rome, he searched hard for me until he found me.
May the Lord grant that Onesiphorus will find mercy from the Lord on that day. And you know very well how many ways he helped me in Ephesus.

2.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: So you, my son, be strong in the grace that is in Christ Jesus.
Take the things you heard me teach in front of many witnesses, and pass them on to faithful people who will be able to teach others too.
Share in the suffering, like a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
No soldier on duty gets tangled up in everyday business. He wants to please his commanding officer.
And an athlete is not given the prize unless he competes by the rules.
The hardworking farmer should be the first to get a share of the crops.
Think about what I am saying, and the Lord will help you understand it all.
Remember Jesus Christ, raised from death, born from the family of David. This is the good news I preach,
and because of it I am suffering, even chained up like a criminal. But God's word is not chained.
So I put up with everything for the sake of God's chosen people, so that they too may be saved in Christ Jesus and share his glory forever.
Here is a true saying: If we died with him, we will also live with him.
If we keep going through hard times, we will also rule with him. If we say we do not know him, he will also say he does not know us.
If we are not faithful, he stays faithful, because he cannot be untrue to himself.
Keep reminding people of these things. Warn them before God not to argue about words. Such arguing does no good; it only ruins those who listen.
Do your best to be the kind of worker God can approve, one who has no reason to be ashamed and who correctly teaches the word of truth.
Stay away from godless, empty talk, because those who do it will go further and further away from God.
Their teaching will spread like a disease that eats the body. Two of them are Hymenaeus and Philetus,
who have wandered away from the truth. They say that the resurrection has already happened, and they destroy the faith of some people.
But God's solid foundation stands firm. It is marked with these words: The Lord knows those who are his, and: Everyone who calls on the name of the Lord must turn away from sin.
In a large house there are not only things made of gold and silver, but also things made of wood and clay. Some are for special use, and some for ordinary use.
Those who make themselves clean from what is wrong will be like the special dishes. They will be holy, useful to the Master, and ready to do any good work.
So run away from the wrong desires of youth. Instead, go after right living, faith, love, and peace, together with those who call on the Lord with a pure heart.
Do not have anything to do with foolish, ignorant arguments, because you know they only cause fights.
The Lord's servant must not fight. Instead, he must be kind to everyone, able to teach, and patient.
He must gently correct those who are against him. Maybe God will give them a change of heart, so that they come to know the truth,
and so that they will wake up and escape the trap of the devil, who has caught them to do whatever he wants.

3.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: You should know this: in the last days there will be hard times.
People will love only themselves and love money. They will brag and be proud. They will say evil things about God, disobey their parents, be ungrateful, and care nothing for what is holy.
They will have no love, refuse to forgive, tell lies about others, and have no self-control. They will be cruel and hate what is good.
They will turn against their friends, act without thinking, and be full of pride. They will love pleasure instead of loving God.
They will look religious on the outside, but they will deny its real power. Stay away from such people.
Some of them sneak into homes and gain control over weak people who are loaded down with sins and led along by all kinds of wrong desires.
These people are always learning, but they are never able to understand the truth.
Just as Jannes and Jambres fought against Moses, these people fight against the truth. Their minds are corrupt, and their faith is worthless.
But they will not get very far. Their foolishness will be clear to everyone, just as it was with Jannes and Jambres.
But you have followed my teaching, my way of life, my goals, my faith, my patience, my love, and my steadiness.
You know about the times I was attacked and the suffering I went through in Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra. I went through terrible attacks, but the Lord rescued me from all of them.
In fact, everyone who wants to live a godly life in Christ Jesus will be attacked.
But evil people and fakes will go from bad to worse, fooling others and being fooled themselves.
But as for you, keep on with what you have learned and firmly believe. You know who taught you,
and you know that from the time you were a child you have known the holy Scriptures. They are able to make you wise, leading to salvation through faith in Christ Jesus.
All Scripture is given by God and is useful for teaching, for showing people what is wrong, for correcting them, and for training them to do right,
so that the servant of God may be ready, fully equipped for every good work.

4.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: So, before God and Christ Jesus — who will judge the living and the dead, and who will appear and rule as king — I give you this charge:
Preach the word. Be ready whether the time seems right or not. Correct people, warn them, and encourage them, with great patience and careful teaching.
For the time will come when people will not listen to sound teaching. Instead, they will gather around them teachers who say what their itching ears want to hear.
They will stop listening to the truth and turn to made-up stories.
But you, keep a clear head in everything. Be willing to suffer. Do the work of telling the good news. Carry out all the duties of your service.
As for me, my life is already being poured out like an offering, and the time for me to leave is near.
I have fought the good fight. I have finished the race. I have kept the faith.

Now a crown is waiting for me — the crown given to those who are right with God. The Lord, the fair judge, will give it to me on that day; and not only to me, but to all who have longed for him to appear.
Do your best to come to me soon.
For Demas has left me, because he loved this present world, and he has gone to Thessalonica. Crescens has gone to Galatia, and Titus to Dalmatia.
Only Luke is with me. Get Mark and bring him with you, because he is helpful to me in my work.
I have sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
When you come, bring the coat I left with Carpus in Troas, and my books, especially the parchments.
Alexander the metalworker did me a lot of harm. The Lord will pay him back for what he did.
You too should watch out for him, because he strongly fought against our message.
The first time I defended myself, no one stood with me; everyone left me. I pray that God will not hold it against them.
But the Lord stood with me and gave me strength, so that through me the message could be fully preached and all the nations could hear it. And I was rescued from the lion's mouth.
The Lord will rescue me from every evil attack and will bring me safely into his heavenly kingdom. To him be glory forever and ever. Amen.
Greet Priscilla and Aquila, and the family of Onesiphorus.
Erastus stayed in Corinth, and I left Trophimus sick in Miletus.
Do your best to get here before winter. Eubulus sends you greetings, and so do Pudens, Linus, Claudia, and all the brothers and sisters.
May the Lord be with your spirit. May grace be with you all. Amen.
